title: Introduction
text: version 4.3, possible visualize spatial data stored memory tmap, e.g. objects class packages sf, terra, stars. instance, dataset NLD_dist sf object. choropleth created follows:  However, huge remote data source like visualize? package, tmap.sources possible specify tm_shape(url) url url remote dataset. Currently PMTiles, remote tile-based data sources supported, support remote data sources added later. Obvious candidate file types: GeoParquet, FlatGeobuf, Cloud Optimised GeoTIFF (COG). run tmap.sources, besides tmap, another package required, namely tmap.mapgl, require tmap mode "maplibre":
code: library(tmap) tm_shape(NLD_dist) +     tm_polygons(         fill = "edu_appl_sci",         fill.scale = tm_scale_intervals(style = "kmeans", values = "plasma")) library(tmap.mapgl) library(tmap.sources) tmap_mode("maplibre") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" -> "view" -> "mapbox" -> "maplibre" #> ℹ rotate with `tmap::rtm()`switch to "plot" with `tmap::ttm()`

title: Introduction
text: Important realise internal data processing tmap skipped entirely. getting categoires (categorical scale), retrieving range optimal interval breaks (interval scale) basis complete data possbile, simply take lot computation time (remote datasets may cover trillions objects). Instead, two scales supported: -scale tm_scale_asis, visual values (e.g. color values) already contained data. categorical scale tm_scale_categorical, data contains levels. user specify used mapping, vector levels vector correspoding visual values (e.g. colors). optionally vector corresponding labels (case differ levels). also possible implement numeric scale (interval continuous). developers: pull requests welcome.

title: PMTiles - self created
text: run tmap.sources, besides tmap, another package required, namely tmap.mapgl, require tmap mode "maplibre": order create PMTiles locally, package freestiler required. created PMTiles files can viewed local server, uploaded remote server.
code: library(tmap) library(tmap.mapgl) library(tmap.sources) tmap_mode("maplibre") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" -> "view" -> "mapbox" -> "maplibre" #> ℹ rotate with `tmap::rtm()`switch to "plot" with `tmap::ttm()` library(freestiler)

title: PMTiles - self created
text: sf object like create PMTiles file, think advance like visualize. replicate choropleth introduction vignette, two options: generate color column, use -scale (tm_scale_asis) generate categorical (factor) column use categorical scale (tm_scale_categorical) Let’s : Now can create PMTiles file:
code: # add categorical variable NLD_dist$edu_cat = cut(NLD_dist$edu_appl_sci, breaks = c(0, 21.5, 29.5, 39.8, 51.5, 90))  # get used colors library(cols4all) pal = c4a("plasma", 5) colorNA = c4a_na("plasma")  # add color variable NLD_dist$edu_fill = pal[cut(NLD_dist$edu_appl_sci, breaks = c(0, 21.5, 29.5, 39.8, 51.5, 90), include.lowest = TRUE)] NLD_dist$edu_fill[is.na(NLD_dist$edu_fill)] = colorNA freestile(     NLD_dist,     "NLD_dist.pmtiles",     layer_name = "edu",     min_zoom = 4,     max_zoom = 15 ) #> Creating MLT tiles (zoom 4-15) for 3340 features across 1 layer... #>   Transforming layer 'edu' to WGS84... #>   Parsed 3340 features across 1 layer in 0.0s #>   Zoom  4/15:      1 tiles ... #>                 1 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom  5/15:      1 tiles ... #>                 1 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom  6/15:      4 tiles ... #>                 4 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom  7/15:      6 tiles ... #>                 6 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  8/15:     15 tiles ... #>                15 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom  9/15:     36 tiles ... #>                34 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom 10/15:    105 tiles ... #>               100 encoded (0.0s) #>   Zoom 11/15:    354 tiles ... #>               348 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom 12/15:   1270 tiles ... #>              1217 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom 13/15:   4749 tiles ... #>              4485 encoded (0.1s) #>   Zoom 14/15:  18235 tiles ... #>             16934 encoded (0.3s) #>   Zoom 15/15:  71155 tiles ... #>             65366 encoded (1.0s) #>   Total: 88511 tiles in 1.9s #>   Writing PMTiles archive (88511 tiles) ... #>   PMTiles write: 1.0s #> Created NLD_dist.pmtiles (38.9 MB) #> View with: view_tiles("NLD_dist.pmtiles")

title: PMTiles - self created
code: tm_shape("NLD_dist.pmtiles") +   tm_polygons(fill = "edu_fill") +   tm_add_legend(fill = pal, labels = levels(NLD_dist$edu_cat), type = "polygons", title = "Education") #> Serving tiles at http://localhost:4321 #>   Directory: /home/runner/work/tmap.sources/tmap.sources/vignettes #> Use stop_server() to stop #> No legends available in mode "maplibre" for map variables

title: PMTiles - self created
code: tm_shape("NLD_dist.pmtiles") +   tm_polygons(     fill = "edu_cat",     fill.scale = tm_scale_categorical(values = pal, levels = levels(NLD_dist$edu_cat)),     fill.legend = tm_legend(title = "Education")   ) #> Serving tiles at http://localhost:6621 #>   Directory: /home/runner/work/tmap.sources/tmap.sources/vignettes #> Use stop_server() to stop

title: PMTiles - overture
code: library(tmap) library(tmap.mapgl) library(tmap.sources) tmap_mode("maplibre") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" -> "view" -> "mapbox" -> "maplibre" #> ℹ rotate with `tmap::rtm()`switch to "plot" with `tmap::ttm()`

title: PMTiles - overture
text: OvertureMaps contain large PMTiles. following one buildings, across World. can also use categorical color palette:
code: library(tmap) library(tmap.mapgl) library(tmap.sources) tmap_mode("maplibre") #> ℹ tmap modes "plot" -> "view" -> #> "mapbox" -> "maplibre"  urls = tmap_src_overture()  tm_shape(   urls$building,    bbox = "Amsterdam") + tm_polygons(fill = "#ee7700") + tm_maplibre(zoom = 14) urls = tmap_src_overture() meta = tmap_src_meta(urls$buildings) vars = tmap_src_vars(meta, layer = "building") cats = tmap_src_cats(meta, layer = "building", var = "subtype")  tm_shape(   urls$buildings,   layer = "building",   bbox = "Amsterdam") + tm_polygons(   fill = "subtype",   fill.scale = tm_scale_categorical(     levels    = cats$categories,     values    = cats$palette,     value.na  = "#ffffff",     label.na  = "Unknown"   ),   fill.legend = tm_legend("Building type") ) + tm_maplibre(zoom = 14) #> Input to asJSON(keep_vec_names=TRUE) is a named vector. In a future version of jsonlite, this option will not be supported, and named vectors will be translated into arrays instead of objects. If you want JSON object output, please use a named list instead. See ?toJSON.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Tennekes M (2026). tmap.sources: Data Sources 'tmap'. R package version 0.1, https://github.com/r-tmap/tmap.sources.
code: @Manual{,   title = {tmap.sources: Data Sources for 'tmap'},   author = {Martijn Tennekes},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1},   url = {https://github.com/r-tmap/tmap.sources}, }

title: Data Sources for tmap
text: Provides support variety spatial data sources ‘tmap’, including remote, tiled, streaming formats. Enables use external vector raster data without requiring full data import, facilitating efficient visualization workflows.

what: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
title: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
text: Convenience functions extract specific parts metadata object returned tmap_src_meta.

title: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
code: tmap_src_layers(meta)  tmap_src_vars(meta, layer = NULL)  tmap_src_cats(meta, layer = NULL, var)

title: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
text: meta List. Output tmap_src_meta. layer Character. Layer name. May omitted source exactly one layer; required otherwise. var Character. Variable name within layer.

title: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
text: tmap_src_layers Character vector layer names. tmap_src_vars Data frame columns variable, class, categories (list-column), palette (list-column) variables layer. tmap_src_cats list elements categories (character vector known category values) palette (named character vector hex colours), NULL catalogue entry exists layer.var.

title: Derived accessors for tmap source metadata — tmap_src_accessors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ urls <- tmap_src_overture() meta <- tmap_src_meta(urls$buildings)  tmap_src_layers(meta) #> [1] "building" "building_part"  tmap_src_vars(meta, layer = "building")  tmap_src_cats(meta, layer = "building", var = "subtype") #> $categories #>  [1] "residential" "commercial" ... #> $palette #>  residential  commercial ... #>  "#f4a460"    "#4169e1"  ... } # }

title: Get tmap source metadata — tmap_src_meta
text: Returns known metadata PMTiles source. Overture Maps URLs, layer_vars data frames automatically annotated categories palette list-columns variables known closed value set.

title: Get tmap source metadata — tmap_src_meta
text: x URL local file path PMTiles source.

title: Get tmap source metadata — tmap_src_meta
text: list elements: input, type, tile_type, url, layers, layer_vars, bbox, zoom.  element layer_vars data frame columns variable, class, categories (list), palette (list).

what: Get Overture Maps PMTiles URLs — tmap_src_overture
title: Get Overture Maps PMTiles URLs — tmap_src_overture
text: Returns named list PMTiles URLs Overture themes. release = "latest" (default), version resolved via Overture STAC catalog runtime.

title: Get Overture Maps PMTiles URLs — tmap_src_overture
Get Overture Maps PMTiles URLs — tmap_src_overture
Get Overture Maps PMTiles URLs — tmap_src_overture
text: Named list URLs: addresses, base, buildings, divisions, places, transportation.
